title: Neural Pipeline
subtitle: Production ML inference that stays calm under load
summary: An end-to-end FastAPI + PyTorch service for real-time predictions with observability, caching, and graceful degradation.
problem: Ad-hoc notebooks could not serve predictions reliably. Latency spiked, models drifted, and ops had no visibility.
motivation: I wanted inference to feel like infrastructure — boring, measured, and trustworthy — not a science experiment.
architecture: FastAPI gateway → Redis feature cache → batched PyTorch workers → Postgres audit log. Metrics via Prometheus. Canary deploys with shadow traffic.
challenges: Cold starts on GPU workers during traffic spikes
Feature store consistency between training and serving
performance: p95 latency under 80ms for cached paths
lessons: Document the evaluation loop as carefully as the model

title: ScrapeCraft
subtitle: Ethical scraping toolkit with resilient queues
summary: A Django-powered orchestration layer for polite crawlers, proxy rotation, and structured extraction pipelines.
problem: One-off scrapers broke silently. Teams needed retries, rate limits, and clean schemas without reinventing orchestration.
motivation: Automation should respect sites and still deliver dependable data.
architecture: Django admin + Celery workers + Playwright extractors + S3 artifact store. Schema validation with Pydantic before sink.
performance: Zero data loss on worker restarts

title: Signalboard
subtitle: Quiet analytics for API health
summary: A minimal dashboard that turns FastAPI telemetry into calm, actionable narratives instead of noisy charts.
problem: Teams drowned in metrics. Important regressions hid in chart noise.
motivation: Surfaces should narrate change, not just plot it.
architecture: Ingest OpenTelemetry → SQLite aggregates → Next.js static views generated nightly.
lessons: Static can be plenty for analytics
